cxu_object.claim: Profits require market participation and there is never a perfect entry time so when the market is flat or ranging and no clear short-term setup exists the agent should evaluate macro sentiment and longer-horizon signals to take directional positions with lower leverage and wider stops accepting that the entry will not be perfect but being in the market captures moves that being flat misses entirely

supporting_contexts.text: After tightening trading rules to prevent fee churn, the agent held flat for 12+ hours making zero trades while ETH moved in a range. Zero trades means zero opportunity to capture any edge. The opposite extreme of overtrading is equally unprofitable — you cannot earn from the sidelines.

When no short-term bounce or momentum setup is present, evaluate macro indicators (crypto breadth, fear and greed, cross-asset correlation, funding rates) and longer-horizon signals (15m, 1h) to establish a directional bias. Enter with lower conviction sizing (15-25% of equity), lower leverage (2-3x), and wider trailing stops (3-5%) to give the position room to develop over hours, not minutes.

A flat ranging market is not the absence of opportunity — it is a low-volatility environment where positions can be established cheaply before the next directional move. The edge comes from being positioned before the breakout, not from catching it after it starts.

maxHoursFlat.description: Maximum hours to stay flat before evaluating macro for a longer-horizon entry

flatMarketSizePct.description: Position size (% of equity) for macro-driven entries in flat markets

flatMarketStopPct.description: Trailing stop % for macro-driven positions (wider than bounce trades)

version.change_description: New hypothesis: being in the market beats perfect entries. When flat for too long, take macro-driven positions with low leverage.
